title: Ervatamia subcapitata
intro: Ervatamia subcapitata, also known as the 'Pulau Tioman vine', is a type of plant that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ervatamia subcapitata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Ervatamia subcapitata is a species of plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia. Ervatamia subcapitata is classified as a member of the Apocynaceae family within the order Gentianales. This plant is further classified into the genus Ervatamia and the species subcapitata. The classification of Ervatamia subcapitata is used in botanical and scientific contexts for identification and research purposes.

Ervatamia subcapitata is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
